skill.name: vision-exploration
skill.description: 终局愿景探索。用户抛出一个模糊 idea，AI 主导引导，通过"追问价值 → 挖掘动机 → 推导演化 → 画终局"的链路，帮用户看到未来最远的可能性。不设限，不收敛，纯发散。
skill.summary: AI-guided vision exploration workflow that transforms vague ideas into concrete end-state visions through structured questioning and multi-dimensional scenario building.
skill.author: yunshu0909

security_audit.summary: Static analysis false positives all dismissed. File is pure Markdown documentation describing an AI-facilitated vision exploration workflow. Line 130 backticks are Markdown inline code formatting, not shell execution. Lines 3, 8, 9, 106 contain Chinese text with arrow characters, not cryptographic algorithms. High entropy at line 1 results from Unicode Chinese characters. No executable code, network access, or security risks detected.

content.value_statement: Transform vague ideas into clear future visions through structured AI-guided exploration. This skill helps you see the farthest possibilities without premature convergence on a single solution.
actual_capabilities: Guides users through a structured five-step vision exploration process
Probes value propositions to uncover root problems being solved
Extracts authentic user motivations through interactive questioning
Derives natural evolution paths from minimal viable to end-state
Generates multiple distinct end-state vision HTML mockups
Maintains design system consistency in all output documents
limitations: Designed for forward-looking exploration, not immediate problem-solving
Requires active user participation and confirmation at each step
Depends on Claude Code capabilities for interactive questions and file operations
Output documents are in Chinese matching the skill's language design

use_cases.title: Product Manager Evaluating Feature Potential
use_cases.description: A product manager has a rough feature idea and wants to understand its full potential before committing engineering resources. The skill guides them through exploring multiple divergent end-states.

use_cases.title: Startup Founder Mapping Product Roadmap
use_cases.description: A founder with an existing MVP wants to visualize where a feature could evolve over time, helping inform strategic decisions about resource allocation and market positioning.

use_cases.title: Innovation Team Exploring Breakthrough Directions
use_cases.description: An enterprise team stuck in incremental improvements uses the skill to break out of local optima and discover radically different product archetypes.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Vision Exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: I have an idea for [describe your idea in 1-2 sentences]. Help me explore where this could lead in the future.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a rough concept and wants to see its potential evolution

prompt_templates.title: Feature Evolution Mapping
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm building [specific feature]. Guide me through the full vision exploration process to understand where this could naturally evolve.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has an existing feature and wants to map its evolution path

prompt_templates.title: Product Line Strategic Exploration
prompt_templates.prompt: Our product does [core function]. I want to explore multiple divergent end-state possibilities for this product category.
prompt_templates.scenario: Team exploring strategic directions for an existing product

prompt_templates.title: Breakthrough Innovation Session
prompt_templates.prompt: We're stuck making incremental improvements to [product area]. Help us break out and explore radically different future archetypes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Team needs to escape local optima and discover breakthrough directions

output_examples.input: I want to build an API switching page for managing multiple AI model providers
output: Value proposition refined from 'switching APIs' to 'AI resource management'
Evolution path: manual switch → informed switch → proactive alerts → intelligent auto-management
Six distinct end-state HTML visions: Event Stream, Conversational Interface, Minimal Status Dashboard, Timeline View, Modular Cards, Command Center

output_examples.input: I'm building a task management feature for my team
output: Value proposition: 'Help teams coordinate work without coordination overhead'
Evolution path: task list → context-aware tasks → auto-prioritization → ambient coordination
Multiple end-state visions showing different information architectures and interaction paradigms

best_practices: Invest time in the questioning phase - rushing to visualization yields shallow results
Confirm each step with the user before proceeding to maintain alignment
Ensure end-state visions differ in information architecture, not just layout styling
anti_patterns: Starting UI design without completing value proposition and motivation discovery
Treating layout variations as distinct solution dimensions
Introducing technical feasibility constraints during the exploration phase

faq.question: What AI platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It requires interactive question capabilities and file system access for generating HTML vision documents.

faq.question: How long does a typical exploration session take?
faq.answer: A complete exploration typically spans multiple conversation turns. Each of the five steps requires user confirmation before proceeding. Expect 15-30 minutes for a thorough session.

faq.question: Do I need design skills to use this?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates production-ready HTML documents following your design system. You only need to express your ideas and provide feedback at each step.

faq.question: Can I use this for non-product ideas?
faq.answer: Yes. While designed for product exploration, the methodology applies to any domain where you want to explore future possibilities - services, processes, organizational structures.

faq.question: What format are the output visions?
faq.answer: Each end-state vision is a self-contained HTML file with inline CSS. They are presentation-ready and can be shared directly with stakeholders or used as design references.

faq.question: Why is the skill content in Chinese?
faq.answer: The original skill was authored in Chinese. The methodology is language-agnostic, and the HTML outputs follow the design system regardless of the guide text language.
